steps.title: Use a quick best-fit anchor
steps.content: For a scatterplot line-of-best-fit question, do not try to hit every point. Pick two points near the middle of the cloud. The cluster is centered roughly around $(1,200)$ and $(2.5,350)$, so those are good anchor points for the trend.

steps.title: Estimate the model
steps.content: The slope from those anchor points is $\frac{350-200}{2.5-1}=\frac{150}{1.5}=100$. Using $(1,200)$ in $y=100x+b$ gives $200=100(1)+b$, so $b=100$. The best-fit equation should be close to $y=100x+100$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D has the right slope but a $y$-intercept of $0$, which puts the line too low for small house sizes. Choice A is much too steep, and choice C is too flat. Choice B has slope $100$ and intercept $100$, matching the center of the data cloud. Therefore choice B is correct.
